name: Road to Haza y Castillo de la Vega — circular route via the Duero Route
name_original: Roa a Haza y Castillo de la Vega — circular por la Ruta del Duero
region_intro: Mountain biking around Roa offers a network of trails primarily traversing the Duero river basin and surrounding agricultural lands. The region is characterized by rolling hills, riverine paths, and areas of woodland, with elevations generally under 500 meters. Routes often connect historical villages and castles, providing varied scenery for mountain bikers. The terrain is suitable for a range of abilities, from moderate to more challenging rides. Best mountain bike trails around Roa The most popular mountain bike trail is Road to Haza y Castillo de la Vega — circular route via the Duero Route, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This circular route follows the Duero River and passes by historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Path alongside the Duero – Aranda Bridge loop from Roa, a moderate 37.6 miles (60.6 km) path. This route offers extensive views along the Duero River. Local mountain bikers also love the Haza and its castle – Haza Walled Village loop from Fuentelisendo, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail leading through historical villages and past a castle, often completed in about 2 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Roa is defined by river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and scattered woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult challenges. The routes in Roa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Roa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Roa?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Roa, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region's network primarily traverses the Duero river basin, rolling hills, and agricultural lands.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Roa?
A: Yes, while most trails are moderate, there is at least one easy mountain bike route available. For families or beginners, look for shorter, less challenging paths that stick to flatter riverine sections. The overall terrain is generally rolling hills with elevations under 500 meters, making many routes accessible.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Roa?
A: Mountain biking in Roa features a mix of terrain, including paths along the Duero River, trails through agricultural landscapes, and scattered woodlands. You'll encounter rolling hills and some historical villages, with elevations generally staying below 500 meters.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult mountain bike routes around Roa. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path alongside the Duero – Peñafiel Castle loop from Fuentecén&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 100 km long and features significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in the Roa area?
A: Yes, many routes around Roa are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188030560/road-to-haza-y-castillo-de-la-vega-circular-route-via-the-duero-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Haza y Castillo de la Vega — circular route via the Duero Route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17542072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza and its castle – Haza Walled Village loop from Fuentelisendo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often connect historical villages and castles. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza and its castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4384200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Zúñiga Family&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5859939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza Walled Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188030560/road-to-haza-y-castillo-de-la-vega-circular-route-via-the-duero-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Haza y Castillo de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; route specifically passes by historical sites.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Roa?
A: The mountain bike routes in Roa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Riders often appreciate the varied scenery, which includes river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any trails that offer extensive views?
A: Yes, routes that follow the Duero River often provide extensive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17542488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path alongside the Duero – Aranda Bridge loop from Roa&lt;/a&gt; is known for offering broad vistas along the river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Roa?
A: The region's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler, wetter periods.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that go through woodlands?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate woodland sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187997768/vuelta-por-el-bosque-de-la-horra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta por el bosque de La Horra&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you through the forest of La Horra, offering a different riding experience.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails around Roa typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187997768/vuelta-por-el-bosque-de-la-horra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta por el bosque de La Horra&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour, while longer, more challenging trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path alongside the Duero – Peñafiel Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; can take over 8 hours.
tips: Haza, a small walled town on top of a hill, overlooks the Riaza plain. A perfect place to stop while mountain biking.
Beautiful medieval bridge that crosses the Duero river on the outskirts of the town of Roa de Duero. It stands out for its three large semicircular arches.

waypoints.name: Medieval Bridge of Roa de Duero
